texto: She didn’t believe there was anything like perfect, fairytale love. Most people were flawed and prone to mistakes. She thought herself a romantic but treated love with the same practicality she did most things. They’d had fall-outs and misunderstandings but she’d never been free to be herself like she was with Edward. She couldn’t imagine being with another person after him.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Myne Whitman: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “She didn’t believe there was anything like perfect, fairytale love. Most people were flawed and p…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que motivacao, emotional, inspiracional pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de inspiracao e inspiracional.

frase_original: She didn’t believe there was anything like perfect, fairytale love. Most people were flawed and prone to mistakes. She thought herself a romantic but treated love with the same practicality she did most things. They’d had fall-outs and misunderstandings but she’d never been free to be herself like she was with Edward. She couldn’t imagine being with another person after him.
